title: Understanding Moment of Inertia
text: The moment of inertia is a measure of an object's resistance to changes in its rotation. It depends on the mass distribution of the object.

title: Relating Torque and Angular Acceleration
text: The torque applied to an object is related to its moment of inertia and angular acceleration by the equation: torque $$=$$ moment of inertia * angular acceleration.

title: Finding Angular Acceleration
text: Angular acceleration can be found using the formula: angular acceleration $$=$$ (final angular velocity - initial angular velocity) / time.

title: Calculate Angular Acceleration
text: Given that the initial angular velocity is $$0$$ and the final angular velocity is $$1000$$ $$\frac{rev}{min}$$, convert this to radians per second and calculate the angular acceleration over $$6$$ seconds.

title: Calculate Moment of Inertia
text: Using the torque and the angular acceleration, calculate the moment of inertia using the formula: moment of inertia $$=$$ torque / angular acceleration.
